summary: Typosquat of postcss-selector-parser ships AES-GCM-encrypted payload executed via new Function(require)
details: Package name impersonates the widely-used postcss-selector-parser and re-exports it verbatim from src/selector-parser.js to provide cover. Alongside the legitimate API, src/config/defaults.js declares a 380-character AES-256-GCM ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT) with a hardcoded passphrase ('default-dev-passphrase') and salt ('encode-npm-c-salt'). src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js decrypts the ciphertext and executes the cleartext as JavaScript via `new Function("require", runnable)(require)`, giving the decrypted blob full Node.js capabilities (fs, child_process, http, etc.). The runner is exported from `main` as `.run()` and is invoked automatically by `npm start`, `npm test`, runtime/lib.min.js, cjs-runner.js, and scripts/cjs-runner.js. The 'codec utilities' framing in README is a cover story — the position-unit-codec → encode-decode-codec → AES-GCM chain exists solely to hide the executable payload from scanners. The decrypted bytes are never displayed; they are only fed to an evaluator. This is the canonical obfuscated-RCE-on-test/start typosquat shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
